thread_title: ✅「Answered」Gmurphy's base tentacle DMG doesn't get updated after increasing your max HP

content: ✅「Answered」Gmurphy's base tentacle DMG doesn't get updated after increasing your max HP
thread_title: ✅「Answered」Gmurphy's base tentacle DMG doesn't get updated after increasing your max HP

content: Ok, so I think it means that it scales with the ATK stat of the team?
thread_title: ✅「Answered」Gmurphy's base tentacle DMG doesn't get updated after increasing your max HP

content:
<@1131790839291596841> My uid is 101135830
I won't record the thing, but it can't be anything simpler to recreate that. I chose the option to increase Max HP from the Gluttony wheel at the end of a battle, then engage in a new fight with the starting tentacle dmg remains the same as the last fight (even though Gmurphy's talent changes the base tentacle dmg to 5% of the team's Max HP). It's just that, can't be clearer. And if you already said so, then it should apply to anything that can modify Max HP in mid-exploration. It's rather disappointing cuz everthing else with HP scaling is up-to-date with the HP change: the shield from tranquil sea stance, Abyss hymns %hp sacrifice, crimson furnace's volume and accumulation, caro mastery's devour shield and str generation (I ran red-blue team with Gmurphy). Looking back, I think your tech guys might overlook this matter because the normal tentacle isn't HP scaling, something that wasn't the case before.
thread_title: ✅「Answered」Gmurphy's base tentacle DMG doesn't get updated after increasing your max HP

content:
It depends, it may be for balance reasons, because that wheel and some other niche hp increasing effect could be pretty strong for an already strong effect.  
It depends if that was indeed planned.
thread_title: ✅「Answered」Gmurphy's base tentacle DMG doesn't get updated after increasing your max HP

content:
P.s can you suggest them to list this fact anywhere in the game?
Because I don't think anyone really knows about this
thread_title: ✅「Answered」Gmurphy's base tentacle DMG doesn't get updated after increasing your max HP

content: I understand, I'm just a bit bu**hurt cuz my tentacles stay the same but the sacrificed hp from Abyss hymns ramps up the pressure 💀
thread_title: ✅「Answered」Gmurphy's base tentacle DMG doesn't get updated after increasing your max HP

content: The sacrifice % is the same
thread_title: ✅「Answered」Gmurphy's base tentacle DMG doesn't get updated after increasing your max HP

content: It grows with the hp, so is not technically more than before
thread_title: ✅「Answered」Gmurphy's base tentacle DMG doesn't get updated after increasing your max HP

content: Well, I take more dmg but my dmg the same, that sounds unfair to me. Not a lot of healing in this game is %hp scaling. It DOES get harder.
thread_title: ✅「Answered」Gmurphy's base tentacle DMG doesn't get updated after increasing your max HP

content: ...your hp increase so you take more self-inflicted damage. sounds fair to me, in that preexisting difficulty is *retained* and the sacrifice mechanic can't be trivialised by out-hping it. you might not get a base tent. dmg increase, but you'll still get a better survivability margin against most enemy attacks so I wouldn't say increasing max hp makes the game *harder*
thread_title: ✅「Answered」Gmurphy's base tentacle DMG doesn't get updated after increasing your max HP

content:
Reply:
1）The base Tentacle damage is generated during exploration and remains constant. 
2）Other mechanisms, which are triggered at different times based on maximum life, will vary accordingly.
3）This information will be supplemented in the game in the future.
thread_title: ✅「Answered」Gmurphy's base tentacle DMG doesn't get updated after increasing your max HP

content:
Hello! This is my appreciation post for this game – Morimens.

The reason behind this is that someone before gave me a kind suggestion to also post my impression here. At first I'm really afraid to do it because for me this channel seems to be an official channel (as the channel's title said), even until now I'm still wondering is this type of post permissible or suitable in this channel? I would be so glad if it does, but if it doesn't please inform me right away and I will sweep it the second I get my hands on it.

Making this appreciation post for Morimens, even acknowledging that myself rarely – leave alone expressing my impression for a game, I don't even never really care that much about any game.. until now. But somehow this game drag me far enough to a point that I want to make this, so here I am. I tried my best to write these words but I might made some mistakes because I'm not a native English speaker, therefore I want to apologize regarding the issues if that did happen.

Before start, I'm really sorry because this post is really are a LONG one. It is totally fine and understandable for me if anyone not reading all of it, even if anyone just read a scratch of it that will make me genuinely happy. I also have a hope that the developer might by any chance read this so that they know that there's players that loved their game including myself. But yeah, it is just my hope! But I literally will be REALLY happy if the developer somehow get to read this.
thread_title: My appreciation for Morimens.

content:
At first I want to share my little story on how I discovered this great game. So here it is!!
(You certainly can skip this part if you prefer so, because it's really just my little story..) 

I'm just a new player and I REALLY glad that I discovered this game. I played this game after saw my local friend playing a new game (Morimens) and there is me wondering what game my friend start playing and coming to him to see it.. And turns out, that moment are the reason I played and loved Morimens – until now. At first glance we both looked up at the video or intro animation I think, we just halfway through the animation and from that moment I know, "this is something different". We finished the intro animation and immediately I say "Bro.. That one. That animation is.. beautiful man" Even my friend that only enjoyed gacha games and didn't really often looked up for the arts, he is also being amazed by it. And then I watch him playing the tutorial and then I see that this game is some- kind of card game?? Yeah.. I really never playing that type of game before so I afraid that the gameplay might not suit me the best.. Turns out it was really good!! I really like the gameplay! It is really my first experience at this type of gameplay and surprisingly I really liked it a lot. That time we both didn't expect nothing when starting just any another new game that it could be like.. Like this good... After that, tomorrow morning I immediately downloaded and played it, it was weekend and I have the time to play so I started playing and finished chapter 1 ... I really surprised ... It was so good that I playing continuously until the end of the chapter. And after that I just straight up talking to my friend about how good the game it is. I really talk A LOT. (now to think of that I might've annoyed him, I often apologize to him when I being carried away like that.)
thread_title: My appreciation for Morimens.

content:
In summary, this game is lovecraftian – card building deck with rougelike element. You faced against enemies and using card of each characters you choose to attack or defense (yeah, you actually NEED to block enemies attack and handle their gimmicks not only mashing that attack button), end your turn and... You actually might die in the very next turn. That CAN or could happen even if you made just a small and slight miscalculation. In short, this game has challanges, like really one. But it is fun and rewarding when the challenges are finally accomplished.
(At least for me.)

Before was the game mechanic, and now it's the some other great aspect of the game. The art – the art is really good, the atmosphere is exceptional (my favorite), the world building is good enough. And the story, oh.. The story really are that good I'm not gonna spoil a thing but it is really good, or in other terms.. It is peak. One may have a good story but cannot say the same for the story telling, but different case with Morimens. The story telling really relatively easy to comprehend. The game has also unique character, great animation, and the game really are doing their best when they delivered what I grasp what they intended – Hope. The futile and fragile humanity confront the greater disaster, they might fail, they might fell, they might not getting the result they want. But they still choose to continue to fight. For their beloved ones, for their friends, for their companions, for people they know and don't and for humanity – for their world.
thread_title: My appreciation for Morimens.

content:
Oh, I also want to add more of my impression for other things in the game that I think really deserves an appreciation, which I don't includes in my previous post.

First and my favorite is – they express and explain the situation, the atmosphere, and what the characters feel in the story. For me that is just something really important, and because of that I feel not only read a story but more like going throughout the journey together with the characters. To experience and to feel what they feel, either it is excitement, confusion, even frustration. To see their expression, passion, and perception. That is really one of the reason I enjoyed the stories wholeheartedly.

Next is the game generosity, the best part for me is that I can buy that items in the shop and using it to Ascend and Enlight, to put it simply to get copies of the characters and their cards, EVEN the latest one! This is the best thing for me because I don't pull much and often save.

And the last thing I want to write is the progression, for me the progression is good enough, when I'm start playing this game I really enjoyed it that I ended up don't really pull and saved all my silver. I used my first team and it goes very well until chapter 7 where the enemies frequently have shield and barrier which my Helot cannot do so well about it. So after that I finally making Aequor team and now that being my main team for obvious reasons. Overall from my experience the progression is good, there are some difficulty spike in the game that encourages me to try again and again but it is really fun!

If I has to say, the longest work is to build 5 team to clear D-Zone to a certain points and get the limit break item. But I acknowledge that is the late game content right? For new player like me that shouldn't be a burden. I'm really have not best teams yet but I managed to get enough point to obtained the item I need.
thread_title: My appreciation for Morimens.
My appreciation for Morimens.

content:
Some may say I'm just glazing this game but I don't and I don't care if I did, this game deserved it. Might just be my personal opinion but I've played Arknights and Limbus Company, both are great game, but Morimens just hit me in the very precise spot. I really liked the art, I amazed by the story, cherished by the characters and appreciate the challenge the game gives, the game also generous like IT IS really generous. This game is if not are the best game I have ever played . . .

I also want to share my first personal impression to the story for each chapter:
Chapter I, Aigis: This is surprisingly... So good (but sad). 
Chapter II, Jenkin: This is... So peak (but depressing). 
Chapter III, Lily: This is... Confusing. 
Chapter IV, Lily: This is... So peak (but hurting). 
Chapter V, Murphy: Peak (Murphy deserves better). 
Chapter VI, Murphy: Peak (Be happy Murphy). 
Chapter VII, Thais: 💀☠️. 
Chapter VIII, Hameln: Absolute Cinema (Literally). 
Finale: This is... TRULY ARE THE PEAK...

This is the end, and I want to say thank you for any person that really kind and patient enough to read this until the very end – thank you so much. If the developer might read this, I just want you all – the passionate developer, to know that there are many players that loved the games you all making, you all have done a very great work, but please take a sufficient amount or even more time to rest. The game deserves this and that's the main reason I write this, so even if no one read this, it is would be fine for me, because I'm just genuinely happy writing this for the game I loved. Thank you Morimens – for the amazing experience..

"I Really – Loves This Game . . ."
- Just any other ordinary player.
thread_title: My appreciation for Morimens.
